> Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1
> 
> This is fixed in patch https://review.whamcloud.com/#/c/fs/lustre-release/+/40113/

I don't think the patch 40113 is mature enough to be merged immediately. On the contrast, this patch is so simple that personally I'm more confident about it to resolve only LU-16253.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review+1

I like simple patches :)

Patch Set 2:

Rushing is always a bad idea. Its better to do it right. Now I could support this patch if its meant to be backport to older Lustre version.  Please use refcount instead of an atomic. refcounts do proper memory ordering. See

https://docs.kernel.org/core-api/refcount-vs-atomic.html

Patch Set 2:

thanks for the good reading, James.

Patch Set 2:

> Patch Set 2:
> 
> Rushing is always a bad idea. Its better to do it right. Now I could support this patch if its meant to be backport to older Lustre version.  Please use refcount instead of an atomic. refcounts do proper memory ordering. See
> 
> https://docs.kernel.org/core-api/refcount-vs-atomic.html

atomic_t is OK to be used as a reference count. Actually it's even OK to use an additional spinlock to protect a long type of oo_ref. refcount_t may be a better way but not the only right way.

At nrs_orr.c:479 there is an assertion of (refs == 0), which is also the bug this patch trying to fix. For a refcount_t type, 0 may be converted to another special value (in my test it is REFCOUNT_SATURATED or 0xC0000000), and I guess this behavior varies in different versions of kernel because even REFCOUNT_SATURATED itself is not defined in all versions of kernel. So if we insist on refcount_t type, we need to check refs is 0, REFCOUNT_SATURATED, or some other value which is hard to be predicted now.

Patch Set 2: Code-Review-1

This change cannot be cherry-picked to master.
Please rebase the change locally and upload the rebased commit for review.

Abandoned

fixed in https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/40113/

parents.subject: LU-16785 build: Cleanup test for IS_ENCRYPTED macro

commit.subject: LU-16253 ptlrpc: define nrs_orr_object.oo_ref atomic_t
commit.message:
LU-16253 ptlrpc: define nrs_orr_object.oo_ref atomic_t

nrs_orr_object.oo_ref is a reference counter but not atomic type.
nrs_trr_hash_ops.hs_put() is filled with nrs_orr_hop_put(), which
decreases oo_ref without any protection. So change it to atomic_t
to eliminate any potential race condition.

Signed-off-by: Lei Feng <flei@whamcloud.com>
Test-Parameters: trivial testlist=sanityn env=ONLY=77d,ONLY_REPEAT=100
Change-Id: I69d27eebdddab79d7dd7e279391cd841e438b5d3
